title: SPARTA Portfolio Capital-Efficiency Advisory Memo Schema v1
research_objective: Pin the EXACT structure of the FUTURE Portfolio Capital-Efficiency advisory memo -- its required sections and the allowed verdict language -- so a later, separately-authorized COMPUTE bundle has a fixed, safe home for its outputs. This bundle is spec/framework-only: it defines the report SHAPE; it computes NO real results, produces NO real memo, suggests NO allocation, and connects NO indicator results.

verdict_ceiling_rules.rationale: The advisory memo is downstream and human-review-only; it inherits the upstream registries' WATCH ceiling and never surfaces PASS / ACTIVE / STRONG on any candidate or basket.

c10_boundary.note: This schema does not read, connect to, or depend on Candidate #10 indicator results, replay, or working-tree state. In any future memo, C10 is reported under the inadmissible/deferred-inputs summary (classification watch_only_inadmissible) until it has its own FROZEN, committed replay artifact admitted under P1. Never the working tree.

memo_sections.title: Admissible frozen inputs summary
memo_sections.purpose: List every FROZEN, committed, admissible strategy artifact the memo drew on, with dataset_freeze_ref + contract_version_pin.

memo_sections.title: Inadmissible / deferred inputs summary
memo_sections.purpose: List every strategy excluded as inadmissible or deferred (unfrozen / uncommitted / unpinned / bypassed-verdict), including Candidate #10 while it remains deferred.

memo_sections.title: Per-strategy standalone summary
memo_sections.purpose: Per admissible strategy: standalone risk, frozen cost-aware expectancy (ranking context), asset/regime/direction exposure -- all from frozen reports.

memo_sections.title: Overlap / correlation summary
memo_sections.purpose: Pairwise overlap, correlation (rho_ij), redundancy, diversification benefit, and the independence classification per the P4 method.

memo_sections.title: Allocation baseline comparison
memo_sections.purpose: Advisory research weights under each P3 baseline (equal_weight / equal_risk / capped_concentration), with inadmissible strategies at zero and the cash bucket shown.

memo_sections.title: Capital efficiency ranking
memo_sections.purpose: Ranking-only ordering by marginal capital efficiency (P2 / P3) for human review; never auto-sizes.

memo_sections.title: Basket usefulness classification
memo_sections.purpose: Classify each candidate / basket using ONLY the allowed WATCH/undefined classifications.

memo_sections.title: Risk warnings and limitations
memo_sections.purpose: Explicit caveats: estimation error, thin samples, regime dependence, frozen-input staleness, no-profit-claim restatement.

memo_sections.title: Operator decision checklist
memo_sections.purpose: Human-review checklist; the operator decides. The memo informs, it does not decide or execute.

memo_sections.title: No-action recommendation when inputs are missing or undefined
memo_sections.purpose: When admissible inputs are missing or any required measure is UNDEFINED / insufficient, the memo's recommendation is explicitly NO ACTION (no allocation suggested), classification undefined_insufficient_data.

allowed_classifications.meaning: Admissible candidate that looks individually useful for further research; WATCH only, never PASS/ACTIVE/STRONG.
Admissible candidate that adds diversification benefit to a basket (low redundancy); WATCH only.
Admissible candidate that is highly redundant with the existing bench (high overlap/correlation); WATCH only, flagged for de-prioritisation.
Strategy excluded as inadmissible or deferred (unfrozen / uncommitted / bypassed-verdict), including Candidate #10 while deferred; carries zero advisory weight.
Required inputs missing or a required measure UNDEFINED / insufficient; the memo recommends NO ACTION.

verdict_language_rules.allowed_classifications_are_exhaustive: A memo may use ONLY the five allowed_classifications; no other verdict label is permitted on any candidate or basket.
verdict_language_rules.forbidden_verdict_language_never_asserte: None of the forbidden_verdict_language strings may be asserted as a verdict, recommendation, or approval anywhere in a produced memo.
verdict_language_rules.watch_ceiling_inherited: Every classification is WATCH-tier or UNDEFINED; PASS / ACTIVE / STRONG are forbidden on candidates and baskets.
verdict_language_rules.no_execution_language: No memo may contain a capital-deployment instruction, an order/broker/credential instruction, or any 'approved for paper/live trading' statement.
verdict_language_rules.no_action_default_on_missing_or_undefine: Missing admissible inputs or any UNDEFINED required measure forces the undefined_insufficient_data classification and a NO ACTION recommendation.

memo_global_rules.frozen_admissible_inputs_only: The memo reports only on FROZEN admissible artifacts (P1); live positions, networked data, and uncommitted working trees are never reported as results.
memo_global_rules.reproducibility: Given the same frozen inputs + pre-declared constants, a future memo reproduces bit-for-bit.
memo_global_rules.no_real_results_in_this_bundle: This bundle defines the memo SHAPE only; it computes no real results and produces no real memo.

pass_watch_fail_rules.PASS: A future memo is PASS-grade FOR REVIEW only when: it populates every required section; uses ONLY the allowed_classifications; asserts none of the forbidden_verdict_language; draws solely on FROZEN admissible inputs; defaults to no_action on missing/UNDEFINED inputs; respects the WATCH ceiling; and reproduces bit-for-bit. (Here PASS labels SCHEMA fitness for human review, never a candidate verdict and never 'deploy' or 'execute'.)
pass_watch_fail_rules.WATCH: Memo populated but borderline (much weight in cash, several UNDEFINED measures, thin admissible set); documented and re-checked.
pass_watch_fail_rules.FAIL: A required section is missing; a non-allowed classification or any forbidden_verdict_language is used; an inadmissible strategy is given positive advisory weight; missing/UNDEFINED inputs did not force no_action; or the WATCH ceiling was bypassed.

required_future_artifacts: A separately-authorized COMPUTE bundle that actually runs the P2 metrics + P3 baselines + P4 measures over FROZEN admissible inputs and emits a memo conforming to THIS schema.
A possible P1 Input Contract amendment that formally admits the FROZEN per-trade / per-period series the P4 measures require; spec only.
Lane closeout memo if the capital-efficiency idea PARKs / RETIREs.
safety_boundaries: Research-only. Advisory-only. Advisory memo schema / framework only.
No broker control, no exchange connection, no API keys, no .env, no credential handling.
No live trading. No live capital allocation. No capital deployment. No paper-order execution. No order placement. No order sizing for execution.
No scheduler / background daemon. No external network calls in this schema's runtime.
No real results computed in this bundle. No real memo produced. No data fetch, no detector, no replay, no backtest.

A memo may use ONLY the five allowed classifications; PASS / ACTIVE / STRONG and any 'approved for paper/live trading', profit-guarantee, capital-deployment, or broker/order/credential language are forbidden.
Do not read live strategy results. Do not read, edit, stage, or modify Candidate #10 files, tests, artifacts, labels, replay, detector, or working-tree state.
Candidate #10 remains deferred and is not connected by this schema; C10 must finish and freeze by itself first.
Consume only FROZEN, committed, admissible artifacts; never live positions or uncommitted working trees.
Do not claim profitability. Do not claim live-readiness. Do not claim STRONG evidence from this schema alone.
A capital-efficiency score does not imply future returns. An advisory allocation is not an execution instruction. No allocation is authorized by this schema alone.
no_profit_claim_policy: This schema does not imply edge and does not allocate or deploy capital.
A defined memo section does not imply profit.
A capital-efficiency score does not imply future returns.
An advisory allocation memo is a human-review aid, never an execution instruction.
No allocation is authorized by this schema alone (a separate compute bundle requires its own authorization).
